Class WidgetBuilder

java.lang.Object
io.github.isoyigido.basic.gui.core.loader.WidgetBuilder
Direct Known Subclasses:
ImageComponent.ImageComponentBuilder, TextComponent.TextComponentBuilder
public abstract class WidgetBuilder extends Object

Stores lists of required and optional widget parameters as Parameter instances. Provides the setParameterValue(String, String) method to parse a String representation from a GUI file and set the value of the parameter tied to the given key. After setting the parameter values, the build() method may be used to build a Widget using the stored parameter values. It returns an Optional containing the built Widget, or an empty Optional if any required parameter does not have a set value or if the provided set of parameter values cannot be used to construct a component.

Subclasses of this class must add required and optional parameters in a parameterless constructor and override the buildComponent() method to provide the component building logic using the added required and optional parameters. A widget declaration in a GUI file which is missing any required parameter will be ignored and the widget will not be added.

Required parameters may be added using addRequiredParameter(String, Parameter) and optional parameters may be added using addOptionalParameter(String, Parameter). Adding parameters using these methods effectively ties the given key in the GUI file to the given parameter. For example, if addRequiredParameter("color", colorParameter) is used, and GUILoader encounters the field color in the widget declaration, the corresponding value will be parsed and set for colorParameter using setParameterValue(String, String).

The default parameterless constructor adds x, y, and anchor required parameters; and layer optional parameter.

To allow usage in GUILoader, subclasses of this class must be annotated with RegisterWidgetBuilder. Subclasses of this class annotated with RegisterWidgetBuilder are registered statically in WidgetBuilderRegistry.

Example usage:
In the example below, the widgets of type button in the GUI file will use a new instance of ButtonBuilder for creation. The button declaration, along with the default parameters, must include width, height, and color parameters; while it could also include a label parameter.

@RegisterWidgetBuilder(type = "button")
public class ButtonBuilder extends WidgetBuilder {
    // - Initialize parameter fields -
    private final IntegerParameter width = new IntegerParameter();
    private final IntegerParameter height = new IntegerParameter();
    private final ColorParameter color = new ColorParameter();
    private final StringParameter label = new StringParameter();

    public ButtonBuilder() {
        // - Add required and optional parameters in the constructor -
        super.addRequiredParameter("width", this.width);
        super.addRequiredParameter("height", this.height);
        super.addRequiredParameter("color", this.color);
        super.addOptionalParameter("label", this.label);
    }

    @Override
    protected Optional<Component> buildComponent() {
        // - Get required parameter values -
        int width = this.width.get();
        int height = this.height.get();
        Color color = this.color.get();

        // Component creation logic with optional parameters...
        Button button = new Button(width, height, color);
        this.label.getOptional().ifPresent(button::setLabel);

        // Return an optional of the component
        return Optional.of(button);
    }
}
See Also:
  • Parameter
  • RegisterWidgetBuilder
  • WidgetBuilderRegistry
  • GUILoader
  • Widget
  • Component
API Note:
Multiple WidgetBuilder implementations may exist for a single Component type. For example, a widget builder of type img-path and a widget builder of type img-resource could both build the same type of image component, while one could load the image from the given path and the other from the given path relative to the resources folder. Furthermore, a single WidgetBuilder implementation may allow for multiple ways to declare the widget by making use of optional parameters. For example, a widget builder of type image may have optional path and resource parameters, and load the image based on whichever one the user uses in the widget declaration. In the case where the user uses neither, or an illegal combination of both, the buildComponent() implementation would return an empty Optional and preferably log a warning. When the buildComponent() method returns an empty Optional, the widget declaration is ignored and the widget is not added.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected final AnchorParameter
    anchor
    The point on the widget anchored to the coordinates (required)
    protected final IntegerParameter
    layerIndex

    protected final IntegerParameter
    x
    The x-coordinate of the widget anchor point (required)
    protected final IntegerParameter
    y
    The y-coordinate of the widget anchor point (required)

  • Field Details

    • x

      protected final IntegerParameter x
      The x-coordinate of the widget anchor point (required)

    • y

      protected final IntegerParameter y
      The y-coordinate of the widget anchor point (required)

    • anchor

      protected final AnchorParameter anchor
      The point on the widget anchored to the coordinates (required)

      protected final IntegerParameter layerIndex
      The layer index of the widget (optional)

  • Constructor Details

    • WidgetBuilder

      protected WidgetBuilder()
      Adds the default required parameters x, y, and anchor; and the optional parameter layer.
      See Also:
      • addRequiredParameter(String, Parameter)
      • addOptionalParameter(String, Parameter)

  • Method Details

    • addRequiredParameter

      protected void addRequiredParameter(String key, Parameter<?> parameter)
      Adds a required widget parameter.
      Parameters:
      key - the key for the parameter in the GUI file (e.g., color)
      parameter - the parameter that is effectively tied to the key
      See Also:
      • Parameter
      API Note:
      Required parameters must have a set value. The build() method ensures that every required parameter has a set value before building the component and creating the widget, and returns an empty Optional if any required parameter does not have a set value. Implementations of buildComponent() can safely use Parameter.get() on a required parameter without checking if the parameter has a set value.

    • addOptionalParameter

      protected void addOptionalParameter(String key, Parameter<?> parameter)
      Adds an optional widget parameter.
      Parameters:
      key - the key for the parameter in the GUI file (e.g., color)
      parameter - the parameter that is effectively tied to the key
      See Also:
      • Parameter
      API Note:
      Optional parameters may not have a set value. Implementations of buildComponent() must use Parameter.getOptional() on optional parameters to get an Optional of the value, or check whether the optional parameter has a value using Parameter.isPresent() before using Parameter.get(). Using Parameter.get() on a parameter that does not have a set value causes an UnsupportedOperationException to be thrown.

    • setParameterValue

      protected void setParameterValue(String key, String valueString)

      Parses the given value string and sets the value of the parameter tied to the given key.

      Special cases:

      • Logs a warning and does nothing if the input valueString is blank
      • Logs a warning and does nothing if the input key is not tied to any parameter
      Parameters:
      key - the key for the parameter in the GUI file (e.g., label)
      valueString - the String representation of the value (e.g., "My Button")
      See Also:
      • Parameter

    • build

      protected Optional<Widget> build()

      Builds a Widget using the stored parameter values.

      Special cases:

      • Logs a warning and returns an empty Optional if any required parameter does not have a set value
      • Returns an empty Optional if buildComponent() returns an empty Optional
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the built Widget, or an empty Optional if any required parameter does not have a set value, or if the provided set of parameter values cannot be used to construct a component
      See Also:
      • Widget

    • buildComponent

      protected abstract Optional<Component> buildComponent()

      Override this method to provide the component building logic using the stored parameters. This method is called in build() after verifying that each required parameter has a set value.

      If the implementation of this method returns an empty Optional, the widget declaration is ignored and the widget is not added.

      See Also:
      • Component
      API Note:
      A single buildComponent() implementation may allow for multiple ways to declare the widget by making use of optional parameters. For example, a widget builder of type image may have optional path and resource parameters, and load the image based on whichever one the user uses in the widget declaration. In the case where the user uses neither, or an illegal combination of both, the implementation would return an empty Optional and preferably log a warning.
